commit_message:
[lite] Add check for bias_size is zero to avoid division by zero. This shouldn't happen for properly converted models. Just safety check

PiperOrigin-RevId: 416383645
Change-Id: If8e508bf696ae8ecfb927e69c139a8ccf7fe60cb
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. An attacker can craft a TFLite model that would trigger a division by zero in `BiasAndClamp` implementation. There is no check that the `bias_size` is non zero. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Prevent `CHECK`-fail when building reference tensor.

The tensor constructor does not allow reference dtypes, as these should not show up explicitly. However, when passed these invalid types instead of building an invalid object the constructor crashes via a `CHECK`-fail. We have a static builder that properly handles this case but is not applicable given current usage.

Instead, before calling the constructor, we can check that the dtype is not a reference type and return an error otherwise, given that the dtype is user controlled so malicious users can trigger denial of service.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409662503
Change-Id: I5892f831fde7f276cd7ab34519cf6b8061c71a59
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. A malicious user can cause a denial of service by altering a `SavedModel` such that Grappler optimizer would attempt to build a tensor using a reference `dtype`. This would result in a crash due to a `CHECK`-fail in the `Tensor` constructor as reference types are not allowed. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: Fix update session when auth fail
cve_desc: Glewlwyd 2.0.0, fixed in 2.6.1 is affected by an incorrect access control vulnerability. One user can attempt to log in as another user without its password.

commit_message:
Handle invalid inputs instead of crashing.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409549744
Change-Id: I7f5935b34b53f7e426a5462fcc027bdbf5dcda24
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. TensorFlow's type inference can cause a heap out of bounds read as the bounds checking is done in a `DCHECK` (which is a no-op during production). An attacker can control the `input_idx` variable such that `ix` would be larger than the number of values in `node_t.args`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. This is the only affected version.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. An attacker can craft a TFLite model that would trigger a division by zero in `BiasAndClamp` implementation. commit_message:
Prevent null dereference read in `GetInitOp`.

We have a map of maps. We test that the key exists in the first map but then we don't have any validation that this also means the second map has the needed key. In the scenarios where this is not the case, we'll dereference a nullptr, if we don't have this check

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408739325
Change-Id: If9bb7ed759aba1f3b56a34913f209508dbaf65ce
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `GetInitOp` is vulnerable to a crash caused by dereferencing a null pointer. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Fix memory leak when a graph node is invalid.

If a graph node is invalid but a kernel is created then we set the kernel back to `nullptr` but we forget to delete it. Hence, we get a memory leak.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408968108
Change-Id: I1d8a9d0d8988ed5e08be8b9f2004ce1b4cd11b7c
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. If a graph node is invalid, TensorFlow can leak memory in the implementation of `ImmutableExecutorState::Initialize`. Here, we set `item->kernel` to `nullptr` but it is a simple `OpKernel*` pointer so the memory that was previously allocated to it would leak. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
KVM: nSVM: always intercept VMLOAD/VMSAVE when nested (CVE-2021-3656)

If L1 disables VMLOAD/VMSAVE intercepts, and doesn't enable
Virtual VMLOAD/VMSAVE (currently not supported for the nested hypervisor),
then VMLOAD/VMSAVE must operate on the L1 physical memory, which is only
possible by making L0 intercept these instructions.

Failure to do so allowed the nested guest to run VMLOAD/VMSAVE unintercepted,
and thus read/write portions of the host physical memory.

Fixes: 89c8a4984fc9 ("KVM: SVM: Enable Virtual VMLOAD VMSAVE feature")

Suggested-by: Paolo Bonzini <pbonzini@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Maxim Levitsky <mlevitsk@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Paolo Bonzini <pbonzini@redhat.com>
cve_desc: A flaw was found in the KVM's AMD code for supporting SVM nested virtualization. The flaw occurs when processing the VMCB (virtual machine control block) provided by the L1 guest to spawn/handle a nested guest (L2). Due to improper validation of the "virt_ext" field, this issue could allow a malicious L1 to disable both VMLOAD/VMSAVE intercepts and VLS (Virtual VMLOAD/VMSAVE) for the L2 guest. As a result, the L2 guest would be allowed to read/write physical pages of the host, resulting in a crash of the entire system, leak of sensitive data or potential guest-to-host escape.

commit_message:
Fix Null-pointer dereference in BuildXlaCompilationCache

If ConfigProto is not used, then use the default settings which is to allow all devices.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 420391800
Change-Id: I88161ad7042990aef678e77b597a2fb2c8f815be
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. When building an XLA compilation cache, if default settings are used, TensorFlow triggers a null pointer dereference. In the default scenario, all devices are allowed, so `flr->config_proto` is `nullptr`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Avoid Segfault for scalar shapes.

Calling tensor::FromElementsOp with an empty vector of elements and no type
causes a segfault. We need to let the FromElementsOp know which scalar type it
should have.
Also add back the DynamicBroadcastInDimOp canonicalization patterns, which
previously prevented this bug from happening.
Add a regression test that demonstrates the bug.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 417561444
Change-Id: I6d1d6cfb71aabbad6102422625a00bbe253ac95a
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The `simplifyBroadcast` function in the MLIR-TFRT infrastructure in TensorFlow is vulnerable to a segfault (hence, denial of service), if called with scalar shapes. If all shapes are scalar, then `maxRank` is 0, so we build an empty `SmallVector`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. This is the only affected version.

commit_message:
Revert "NFSv4: Handle the special Linux file open access mode"

This reverts commit 44942b4e457beda00981f616402a1a791e8c616e.

After secondly opening a file with O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT flags,
nfs4_valid_open_stateid() will dereference NULL nfs4_state when lseek().

Reproducer:
  1. mount -t nfs -o vers=4.2 $server_ip:/ /mnt/
  2. fd = open("/mnt/file", O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT|O_CREAT)
  3. close(fd)
  4. fd = open("/mnt/file", O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT)
  5. lseek(fd)

Reported-by: Lyu Tao <tao.lyu@epfl.ch>
Signed-off-by: ChenXiaoSong <chenxiaosong2@huawei.com>
Signed-off-by: Trond Myklebust <trond.myklebust@hammerspace.com>
cve_desc: An issue was discovered in fs/nfs/dir.c in the Linux kernel before 5.16.5. If an application sets the O_DIRECTORY flag, and tries to open a regular file, nfs_atomic_open() performs a regular lookup. If a regular file is found, ENOTDIR should occur, but the server instead returns uninitialized data in the file descriptor.

commit_message:
Revert "NFSv4: Handle the special Linux file open access mode"

This reverts commit 44942b4e457beda00981f616402a1a791e8c616e.

After secondly opening a file with O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT flags,
nfs4_valid_open_stateid() will dereference NULL nfs4_state when lseek().

Reproducer:
  1. mount -t nfs -o vers=4.2 $server_ip:/ /mnt/
  2. fd = open("/mnt/file", O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT|O_CREAT)
  3. close(fd)
  4. fd = open("/mnt/file", O_ACCMODE|O_DIRECT)
  5. lseek(fd)

Reported-by: Lyu Tao <tao.lyu@epfl.ch>
Signed-off-by: ChenXiaoSong <chenxiaosong2@huawei.com>
Signed-off-by: Trond Myklebust <trond.myklebust@hammerspace.com>
cve_desc: An issue was discovered in fs/nfs/dir.c in the Linux kernel before 5.16.5. If an application sets the O_DIRECTORY flag, and tries to open a regular file, nfs_atomic_open() performs a regular lookup. If a regular file is found, ENOTDIR should occur, but the server instead returns uninitialized data in the file descriptor.

commit_message:
Replace faulty overflow check with a builder for `TensorShape`.

Prevents an integer overflow that was not caught before.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 415381595
Change-Id: I76585ddedc912bd9f4a390aeafa8e2ced1a28863
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `AddManySparseToTensorsMap` is vulnerable to an integer overflow which results in a `CHECK`-fail when building new `TensorShape` objects (so, an assert failure based denial of service). We are missing some validation on the shapes of the input tensors as well as directly constructing a large `TensorShape` with user-provided dimensions. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Validate `proto.dtype()` before calling `set_dtype()`.

This prevents a `DCHECK`-fail when the proto contains an invalid dtype for a tensor shape with 0 elements or for an incomplete tensor shape.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408369083
Change-Id: Ia21a3e3d62a90d642a4561f08f3b543e5ad00c46
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. When decoding a tensor from protobuf, a TensorFlow process can encounter cases where a `CHECK` assertion is invalidated based on user controlled arguments, if the tensors have an invalid `dtype` and 0 elements or an invalid shape. This allows attackers to cause denial of services in TensorFlow processes. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message:
Handle typeof applied to empty in InstSimplify

Summary:
Do not simplify `typeof` if it is applied to an invalid type. This
handles a case like the one in the added test, where `typeof` is called
on a literal empty in unreachable code.

Reviewed By: kodafb

Differential Revision: D31000173

fbshipit-source-id: 2d7f69cbcc9c1bb0a916585c07171089444c85dc
cve_desc: A type confusion vulnerability could be triggered when resolving the "typeof" unary operator in Facebook Hermes prior to v0.10.0. Note that this is only exploitable if the application using Hermes permits evaluation of untrusted JavaScript. Hence, most React Native applications are not affected.

Prevent a crash due to heap OOB write in grappler.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408318417

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. TensorFlow is vulnerable to a heap OOB write in `Grappler`. The `set_output` function writes to an array at the specified index. Hence, this gives a malicious user a write primitive. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: Fix incorrect value reading in JIT.
cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability was discovered in the PCRE2 library in the compile_xclass_matchingpath() function of the pcre2_jit_compile.c file. This involves a unicode property matching issue in JIT-compiled regular expressions. The issue occurs because the character was not fully read in case-less matching within JIT.

commit_message:
Prevent null pointer dereference in constant folding.

Under certain conditions, an invalid protobuf saved model with invalid nodes would be loaded. During optimization phase, Grappler optimizer will then dereference a null pointer.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409683530
Change-Id: I1f10340a7ec384bc9bc587300390f1078cf5caa0
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: Fix incorrect value reading in JIT.
cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability was discovered in the PCRE2 library in the compile_xclass_matchingpath() function of the pcre2_jit_compile.c file. This involves a unicode property matching issue in JIT-compiled regular expressions. The issue occurs because the character was not fully read in case-less matching within JIT.

commit_message:
Prevent copying uninitialized data in `AssignOp`.

This prevents harder to debug undefined behaviors that cannot be traced back to the original tensor after assignments occur earlier in the graph execution. Several of these undefined behaviors are just reference bindings to null pointers, which are caught when running under ubsan/asan.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408654780
Change-Id: Iad2ec40d43f5fd7ea016c20283356c12d5ddeab1
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `AssignOp` can result in copying uninitialized data to a new tensor. This later results in undefined behavior. The implementation has a check that the left hand side of the assignment is initialized (to minimize number of allocations), but does not check that the right hand side is also initialized. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: codegen.c: fixed a bug in hash code generation with `!val`.
cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in Homebrew mruby prior to 3.2.

commit_message:
Add a check for Key being scalar tensor for MapStage and OrderedMapStage ops.

According to documentation[1][2], key must be int64 value, but this wasn't enforced and the ops would fail with check failure for non-scalar key value.

[1]https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/cc/class/tensorflow/ops/ordered-map-stage
[2]https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/cc/class/tensorflow/ops/map-stage

PiperOrigin-RevId: 413822112
Change-Id: I9d118faf990e6361900aa32272eff486ad9f0e2e
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `MapStage` is vulnerable a `CHECK`-fail if the key tensor is not a scalar. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

Because we have extra 2 arguments coming (kw and rhs).

commit_message:
Add a check for Key being scalar tensor for MapStage and OrderedMapStage ops.

According to documentation[1][2], key must be int64 value, but this wasn't enforced and the ops would fail with check failure for non-scalar key value.

[1]https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/cc/class/tensorflow/ops/ordered-map-stage

commit_message:
Fix Segfault in Concat V2 shape function.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 412120654
Change-Id: I3ff915faea694f9ad8b00024e9af2de9909011be
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of shape inference for `ConcatV2` can be used to trigger a denial of service attack via a segfault caused by a type confusion. The `axis` argument is translated into `concat_dim` in the `ConcatShapeHelper` helper function. Then, a value for `min_rank` is computed based on `concat_dim`. This is then used to validate that the `values` tensor has at least the required rank. However, `WithRankAtLeast` receives the lower bound as a 64-bits value and then compares it against the maximum 32-bits integer value that could be represented. Due to the fact that `min_rank` is a 32-bits value and the value of `axis`, the `rank` argument is a negative value, so the error check is bypassed. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: When compiler_for_platform fails with a status code other than NOT_FOUND, the error is silently ignored and execution continues past the error check. This means the function proceeds to use the platform and attempt client creation even though compiler lookup failed, which could lead to undefined behavior or crashes depending on downstream assumptions.

step_by_step_execution.description: platform_info.xla_device_metadata() returns null, so we skip the first early return.
Platform is successfully obtained via MultiPlatformManager::PlatformWithId.
xla::Compiler::GetForPlatform fails with a status code that is NOT error::NOT_FOUND (e.g., INTERNAL, UNAVAILABLE, etc.).
The inner if-check for NOT_FOUND does not match, so the function does NOT return. Execution falls through past the entire error-handling block, silently ignoring the compiler error.
Execution continues to create client_options and eventually creates the XlaCompilationCache, despite the compiler lookup having failed. This may lead to crashes or incorrect behavior downstream.

commit_message:
Ensure that bundles have metadata on install

If we have a bundle without metadata we wouldn't properly present
the permissions in the transaction.
cve_desc: Flatpak is a Linux application sandboxing and distribution framework. Prior to versions 1.12.3 and 1.10.6, Flatpak doesn't properly validate that the permissions displayed to the user for an app at install time match the actual permissions granted to the app at runtime, in the case that there's a null byte in the metadata file of an app. Therefore apps can grant themselves permissions without the consent of the user. Flatpak shows permissions to the user during install by reading them from the "xa.metadata" key in the commit metadata. This cannot contain a null terminator, because it is an untrusted GVariant. Flatpak compares these permissions to the *actual* metadata, from the "metadata" file to ensure it wasn't lied to. However, the actual metadata contents are loaded in several places where they are read as simple C-style strings. That means that, if the metadata file includes a null terminator, only the content of the file from *before* the terminator gets compared to xa.metadata. Thus, any permissions that appear in the metadata file after a null terminator are applied at runtime but not shown to the user. So maliciously crafted apps can give themselves hidden permissions. Users who have Flatpaks installed from untrusted sources are at risk in case the Flatpak has a maliciously crafted metadata file, either initially or in an update. This issue is patched in versions 1.12.3 and 1.10.6. As a workaround, users can manually check the permissions of installed apps by checking the metadata file or the xa.metadata key on the commit metadata.

In standalone mode (i.e., acting as a proxy or a transparent proxy), with SSL MITM enabled, e2guardian, if built with OpenSSL v1.1.x, did not validate hostnames in certificates of the web servers that it connected to, and thus was itself vulnerable to MITM attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: SSL_new(ctx) can return NULL on failure (e.g., memory allocation failure), but the return value is never checked. The code immediately passes the potentially-NULL `ssl` pointer to SSL_set_options, SSL_set_mode, SSL_set_connect_state, SSL_set_fd, and SSL_set_tlsext_host_name, leading to a NULL pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: SSL context is created successfully via SSL_CTX_new and stored in `ctx`.
Certificate loading and verification param setup succeeds, execution reaches SSL_new call.
SSL_new(ctx) is called but fails (e.g., due to memory exhaustion) and returns NULL.
SSL_set_options(ssl, SSL_OP_ALL) is called with ssl=NULL, causing a NULL pointer dereference.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions several TensorFlow operations are missing validation for the shapes of the tensor arguments involved in the call. Depending on the API, this can result in undefined behavior and segfault or `CHECK`-fail related crashes but in some scenarios writes and reads from heap populated arrays are also possible. We have discovered these issues internally via tooling while working on improving/testing GPU op determinism. As such, we don't have reproducers and there will be multiple fixes for these issues. These fixes will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick these commits on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: If `filter.dim_size(2)` (patch_depth) is zero (e.g., a malformed or zero-dimension filter tensor), the division `in_depth / patch_depth` results in a division by zero, causing undefined behavior / crash.

`patch_depth` is assigned from filter.dim_size(2).
Division `in_depth / patch_depth` is performed, causing integer division by zero.

vulnerabilities.description: If `in_depth` is 0 and `patch_depth` is non-zero, `num_groups` becomes 0, and subsequent divisions by `num_groups` in the `pre_shuffle` and `post_shuffle` lambdas cause division by zero.

code_snippets:
return {tensor.dim_size(0), tensor.dim_size(1), tensor.dim_size(2),
              num_groups, tensor.dim_size(3) / num_groups};

return {num_groups, tensor.dim_size(0), tensor.dim_size(1),
              tensor.dim_size(2), tensor.dim_size(3) / num_groups};

step_by_step_execution.description: input.dim_size(3) is 0, filter.dim_size(2) is non-zero (e.g. 1).
num_groups is computed as 0 / 1 = 0.
pre_shuffle or post_shuffle lambda is called, which computes tensor.dim_size(3) / num_groups, dividing by zero.

commit_message:
Handle invalid inputs instead of crashing.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409549744
Change-Id: I7f5935b34b53f7e426a5462fcc027bdbf5dcda24
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. TensorFlow's type inference can cause a heap out of bounds read as the bounds checking is done in a `DCHECK` (which is a no-op during production). An attacker can control the `input_idx` variable such that `ix` would be larger than the number of values in `node_t.args`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. This is the only affected version.

vulnerabilities.description: In release builds, DCHECK is a no-op. If input_idx[i] (ix) is greater than or equal to node_t.args_size(), the call to node_t.args(ix) performs an out-of-bounds read on the repeated field. An attacker who can craft a graph where an edge's src_output() exceeds the number of type arguments in the source node's experimental_type can trigger this.

step_by_step_execution.description: Vectors input_nodes and input_idx are initialized with size props_->input_types.size(), filled via iteration over in_edges_.

state.value: vector of Node* pointers from edges

step_by_step_execution.description: For a particular edge, edge->src_output() returns a value that is stored into input_idx[i].

state.value: edge->src_output(), could be any non-negative integer

step_by_step_execution.description: The code checks node->def().has_experimental_type() and that node_t.type_id() != TFT_UNSET, then reads ix = input_idx[i].
DCHECK(ix < node_t.args_size()) is compiled away in release/optimized builds, so no bounds check occurs.
node_t.args(ix) is called with an out-of-bounds index, causing an out-of-bounds read on the protobuf repeated field.

commit_message:
libpq: reject extraneous data after SSL or GSS encryption handshake.

libpq collects up to a bufferload of data whenever it reads data from
the socket.  When SSL or GSS encryption is requested during startup,
any additional data received with the server's yes-or-no reply
remained in the buffer, and would be treated as already-decrypted data
once the encryption handshake completed.  Thus, a man-in-the-middle
with the ability to inject data into the TCP connection could stuff
some cleartext data into the start of a supposedly encryption-protected
database session.

This could probably be abused to inject faked responses to the
client's first few queries, although other details of libpq's behavior
make that harder than it sounds.  A different line of attack is to
exfiltrate the client's password, or other sensitive data that might
be sent early in the session.  That has been shown to be possible with
a server vulnerable to CVE-2021-23214.

To fix, throw a protocol-violation error if the internal buffer
is not empty after the encryption handshake.

Our thanks to Jacob Champion for reporting this problem.

Security: CVE-2021-23222
cve_desc: A man-in-the-middle attacker can inject false responses to the client's first few queries, despite the use of SSL certificate verification and encryption.

commit_message:
Prevent null pointer dereference in constant folding.

Under certain conditions, an invalid protobuf saved model with invalid nodes would be loaded. During optimization phase, Grappler optimizer will then dereference a null pointer.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409683530
Change-Id: I1f10340a7ec384bc9bc587300390f1078cf5caa0
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: If node_map_->GetNode() returns nullptr for conv_left_child or conv_right_child, the code dereferences the null pointer when calling IsReallyConstant(). Unlike the mul children which are null-checked, the conv children are used without any null check.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function entry: node is a Mul op with 2 non-control inputs.
mul_left_child and mul_right_child are fetched and null-checked.
One child is constant, the other is a Conv2D/Conv3D node. Device checks pass.
conv_node->input_size() >= 2 check passes, output count and preservation checks pass.
conv_left_child and conv_right_child are fetched via node_map_->GetNode(), but if the input name references a node not in the map, GetNode returns nullptr.

state.value: nullptr (if node not found in map)

step_by_step_execution.description: IsReallyConstant(*conv_left_child) dereferences conv_left_child which is nullptr, causing a null pointer dereference.

commit_message:
Remove a `DCHECK`-fail, log an error instead.

`DCHECK` in debug mode results in crashes. TensorFlow has had multiple vulnerabilities due to this.

Outside of debug mode, `DCHECK` is a no-op.

A better alternative is to report an error to the log buffer and continue. This should happen both in debug mode and in prod mode.

commit_message: fixed #1895
cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a double-free vulnerability in the ilst_box_read function in box_code_apple.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service, even code execution and escalation of privileges.

vulnerabilities.description: Integer underflow on unsigned `size` when the input `size` is between 1 and 3 (inclusive). Since `size` is `u32` (unsigned 32-bit), subtracting 4 from a value less than 4 wraps around to a very large value (e.g., 0xFFFFFFFF), causing the `while (size)` loop to iterate far beyond the actual available data.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is called with a `size` parameter between 1 and 3 (e.g., size=1). The check `if (!size)` passes since size is non-zero.
The 4-byte AV1 config header fields are read from the bitstream (marker, version, seq_profile, etc.). These reads consume bits from `bs` but do not affect `size`.
The validation check on reserved/marker/version is assumed to pass (attacker can craft the bitstream to satisfy these conditions).
initial_presentation_delay fields are read (4 more bits). Then `size -= 4` is executed. Since size is u32 (unsigned), 1 - 4 wraps around.

The `while (size)` loop begins with the wrapped-around huge size value, causing the loop to process OBUs far beyond the legitimate data boundary, leading to out-of-bounds reads from the bitstream and massive memory allocations.

commit_message:
Prevent null dereference read in `GetInitOp`.

We have a map of maps. We test that the key exists in the first map but then we don't have any validation that this also means the second map has the needed key. In the scenarios where this is not the case, we'll dereference a nullptr, if we don't have this check

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408739325
Change-Id: If9bb7ed759aba1f3b56a34913f209508dbaf65ce
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `GetInitOp` is vulnerable to a crash caused by dereferencing a null pointer. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: When the signature_def map contains the key `kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey`, the code looks up the same key in the `outputs()` map of that signature def. However, the result of this inner `find()` is never checked against `end()`. If the `outputs()` map does not contain `kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey`, the iterator returned is `end()`, and dereferencing it (`->second.name()`) is undefined behavior (out-of-bounds read / null pointer dereference).

step_by_step_execution.description: The function is called with a MetaGraphDef whose signature_def map contains the key kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey, but the corresponding SignatureDef's outputs map does NOT contain kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey.
init_op_sig_it is found successfully (not equal to sig_def_map.end()), so execution enters the if-block.

state.value: valid iterator pointing to the signature def entry

step_by_step_execution.description: The inner find() call on the outputs() map searches for kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey but does not find it, returning an end() iterator.
The end() iterator is immediately dereferenced via ->second.name(), which is undefined behavior (reading past the end of the map's internal storage).

commit_message: fixed #1895
cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a double-free vulnerability in the ilst_box_read function in box_code_apple.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service, even code execution and escalation of privileges.

vulnerabilities.description: When the `size` parameter (or the value from `gf_bs_available`) is between 1 and 3 inclusive, the subtraction `size -= 4` causes an unsigned integer underflow on the u32 variable, wrapping it to a very large value (e.g., 0xFFFFFFFF for size=3). This causes the `while (size)` loop to iterate far beyond the actual available data, leading to out-of-bounds reads from the bitstream and excessive memory allocations.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is called with size=3 (or gf_bs_available returns 3). The check `if (!size)` does not trigger since size is non-zero.

The header fields are read from the bitstream consuming 4 bytes (32 bits): marker(1) + version(7) + seq_profile(3) + seq_level_idx_0(5) + seq_tier_0(1) + high_bitdepth(1) + twelve_bit(1) + monochrome(1) + chroma_subsampling_x(1) + chroma_subsampling_y(1) + chroma_sample_position(2) + reserved(3) + initial_presentation_delay_present(1) + 4 bits = 32 bits total. These reads may already go past available data.
Assuming the reserved/marker/version check passes, `size -= 4` is executed. Since size is u32 with value 3, this underflows to 0xFFFFFFFF.

The `while (size)` loop begins with size = 0xFFFFFFFF, causing the loop to attempt parsing OBUs far beyond the actual data boundary. This leads to out-of-bounds reads from the bitstream and potentially many large memory allocations.

cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a null pointer dereference vulnerability in the gf_isom_get_payt_count function in hint_track.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.description: NULL pointer dereference when accessing the chain trak->Media->information->sampleTable->SampleDescription->child_boxes. None of the intermediate pointers (Media, information, sampleTable, SampleDescription) are checked for NULL before dereferencing. If any of these are NULL, the code crashes.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function is called with a GF_TrackBox *trak where, e.g., trak->Media->information->sampleTable is NULL.
hmhd is assigned from trak->Media->information->InfoHeader. Assume it's a valid pointer with type == GF_ISOM_BOX_TYPE_HMHD and subType == 0.
First check: !hmhd is false and hmhd->type == GF_ISOM_BOX_TYPE_HMHD, so we do not return 0.
Second check: !hmhd is false, !hmhd->subType is true (subType == 0), so we enter the if block.
Code dereferences trak->Media->information->sampleTable, which is NULL, causing a NULL pointer dereference/crash.

commit_message:
Fix segfault on OOM in Conv2D.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 404655317
Change-Id: I33588dbd3f5d0fef980e3c908bf5515a9ee09ce7
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions several TensorFlow operations are missing validation for the shapes of the tensor arguments involved in the call. Depending on the API, this can result in undefined behavior and segfault or `CHECK`-fail related crashes but in some scenarios writes and reads from heap populated arrays are also possible. We have discovered these issues internally via tooling while working on improving/testing GPU op determinism. As such, we don't have reproducers and there will be multiple fixes for these issues. These fixes will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick these commits on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: Division by zero if filter.dim_size(2) (patch_depth) is zero. There is no validation that patch_depth is non-zero before dividing.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function reads in_depth from input tensor dimension 3 and patch_depth from filter tensor dimension 2.
Division in_depth / patch_depth is performed without checking if patch_depth is zero, causing a division-by-zero crash.

vulnerabilities.description: Division by zero when num_groups is 0. If in_depth < patch_depth (and both positive), integer division yields num_groups=0, and subsequent tensor.dim_size(3) / num_groups causes division by zero.

step_by_step_execution.description: in_depth and patch_depth are read from tensor dimensions.
num_groups is computed as in_depth / patch_depth using integer division, yielding 0.
pre_shuffle or post_shuffle lambda is invoked, computing tensor.dim_size(3) / num_groups, which is division by zero.

vulnerabilities.description: Out-of-bounds read on explicit_paddings vector if it has fewer than 6 elements and padding == EXPLICIT. There is no bounds check on the size of explicit_paddings before indexing elements [2] through [5].

step_by_step_execution.description: The function receives explicit_paddings as a vector with no size validation.
When padding == EXPLICIT, elements at indices 2-5 are accessed without checking vector size.
Accessing explicit_paddings[2] through [5] reads out of bounds of the vector, causing undefined behavior.

commit_message: fixed #1900
cve_desc: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via a malformed MP4 file in the svc_parse_slice function in av_parsers.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service, even code execution and escalation of privileges.

vulnerabilities.description: The value `si->pps->sps_id` is read from a previously stored PPS entry (which was populated from attacker-controlled bitstream data) and used as an array index into `avc->sps[]` after adding `GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT`, without any bounds validation. If `sps_id` is large or crafted, the computed index `sps_id + GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT` can exceed the bounds of the `avc->sps` array, leading to an out-of-bounds read/write.

step_by_step_execution.description: A PPS ID is read from the bitstream and validated to be <= 255.
The PPS entry is retrieved: `si->pps = &avc->pps[pps_id]`. The `sps_id` field within this PPS was set by a prior parsing step from attacker-controlled input and is not re-validated here.
The slice_group_count check passes (non-zero), so execution continues.
The SPS is accessed using `si->pps->sps_id + GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT` as the index into the fixed-size `avc->sps` array. There is no bounds check on this computed index.

state.value: si->pps->sps_id + GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT (can exceed avc->sps array bounds)
pointer to out-of-bounds memory relative to avc->sps

step_by_step_execution.description: The out-of-bounds pointer is subsequently dereferenced to read `si->sps->log2_max_frame_num`, `si->sps->frame_mbs_only_flag`, `si->sps->poc_type`, etc., causing out-of-bounds reads from heap/stack memory.

commit_message: fixed #2092
cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in GitHub repository gpac/gpac prior to 1.1.0.

commit_message:
Fix bug #707 cert hostnames not being checked
- only happened when openssl v1.1 is used
cve_desc: e2guardian v5.4.x <= v5.4.3r is affected by missing SSL certificate validation in the SSL MITM engine. In standalone mode (i.e., acting as a proxy or a transparent proxy), with SSL MITM enabled, e2guardian, if built with OpenSSL v1.1.x, did not validate hostnames in certificates of the web servers that it connected to, and thus was itself vulnerable to MITM attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: If SSL_new(ctx) returns NULL (e.g., due to memory allocation failure), the code proceeds to call SSL_set_options, SSL_set_mode, SSL_set_connect_state, SSL_set_fd, SSL_set_tlsext_host_name, and SSL_connect on a NULL pointer without any NULL check, leading to a NULL pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: SSL context (ctx) is successfully created and configured with certificates and verification parameters.
SSL_new(ctx) is called but returns NULL due to an internal failure (e.g., out of memory).
SSL_set_options(ssl, SSL_OP_ALL) is called with ssl == NULL, causing a NULL pointer dereference.

vulnerabilities.description: The function loads CA certificates and sets X509 verification parameters, but never calls SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER, ...) and never checks SSL_get_verify_result(ssl) after SSL_connect. This means the peer certificate is never actually validated, allowing a man-in-the-middle attacker to present any certificate (including self-signed or for a different hostname) and the connection will succeed.

step_by_step_execution.description: SSL context is created and CA certificate paths are loaded successfully.

state.value: valid SSL_CTX with loaded CA certs but verify mode defaults to SSL_VERIFY_NONE

step_by_step_execution.description: X509 verification parameters are set with X509_V_FLAG_TRUSTED_FIRST, but SSL_CTX_set_verify is never called with SSL_VERIFY_PEER.
SSL_connect completes the handshake. Because verify mode is SSL_VERIFY_NONE, the server's certificate is not verified. A MITM attacker can present any certificate.
The function returns 0 indicating success, with isssl set to true, even though the peer was never authenticated.

commit_message:
add explicit format specifier to printf() invocations

CWE-134
cve_desc: Depending on the way the format strings in the card label are crafted it's possible to leak kernel stack memory. There is also the possibility for DoS due to the v4l2loopback kernel module crashing when providing the card label on request (reproduce e.g. with many %s modifiers in a row).

vulnerabilities.description: dev->card_label is used as the format string argument to snprintf. If an attacker can control the contents of dev->card_label (e.g., via an ioctl that sets the card label), they can inject format specifiers like %s, %n, %x, etc., leading to information disclosure, denial of service, or arbitrary memory writes.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function retrieves the loopback device structure from the file handle.
labellen is computed as the minimum of sizeof(cap->card) and sizeof(dev->card_label).
snprintf is called with dev->card_label as the format string. If dev->card_label contains format specifiers (e.g. "%s%s%s%s%n"), snprintf will interpret them, reading from or writing to the stack.

state.value: destination buffer being written to via format string interpretation

commit_message: fixed #1900
cve_desc: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via a malformed MP4 file in the svc_parse_slice function in av_parsers.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service, even code execution and escalation of privileges.

vulnerabilities.description: Out-of-bounds array access when indexing into avc->sps. The value si->pps->sps_id is read from a previously parsed PPS and is not validated before being added to GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT and used as an index into the avc->sps array. If sps_id is large or the sum exceeds the array bounds, an out-of-bounds read/write occurs.

step_by_step_execution.description: pps_id is read from the bitstream and validated to be in range [0, 255].
si->pps is set to &avc->pps[pps_id], which is within bounds. The pps structure was previously populated from parsing and contains a sps_id field that was not re-validated here.

state.value: arbitrary value stored in the PPS, no bounds check performed in this function

step_by_step_execution.description: The code checks si->pps->slice_group_count is non-zero, but does not validate sps_id.

The index si->pps->sps_id + GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT is used to access avc->sps without any bounds checking. If sps_id is crafted to be large, or the sum exceeds the sps array size, this results in an out-of-bounds array access.

state.value: si->pps->sps_id + GF_SVC_SSPS_ID_SHIFT (potentially exceeds avc->sps array bounds)

step_by_step_execution.description: The out-of-bounds pointer si->sps is subsequently dereferenced (e.g., si->sps->log2_max_frame_num), leading to reads from invalid memory.

commit_message:
Prevent null pointer dereference in `mutable_graph_view`

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409684472
Change-Id: I577eb9d9ac470fcec0501423171e739a4ec0cb5c
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: graph.GetNode() can return nullptr if the node referenced by tensor_id.node() is not found in the graph. The returned pointer is dereferenced without a null check, leading to a null pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function checks if the node is an Identity or IdentityNSingleInput with at least one input.

state.value: A NodeDef that is an Identity node with input_size() > 0

step_by_step_execution.description: ParseTensorName extracts the tensor id from node.input(0).
IsTensorIdControlling check passes (returns false), so execution continues.

graph.GetNode is called with tensor_id.node(). If the referenced node does not exist in the graph (e.g., the graph is in an inconsistent state or the input references an external/missing node), GetNode returns nullptr.
input_node is dereferenced via *input_node in IsSwitch(*input_node), causing a null pointer dereference.

commit_message:
run: Don't allow chroot()

If we don't allow pivot_root() then there seems no reason why we should
allow chroot().

Partially fixes GHSA-67h7-w3jq-vh4q.

Signed-off-by: Simon McVittie <smcv@collabora.com>
cve_desc: Flatpak is a system for building, distributing, and running sandboxed desktop applications on Linux. In versions prior to 1.10.4 and 1.12.0, Flatpak apps with direct access to AF_UNIX sockets such as those used by Wayland, Pipewire or pipewire-pulse can trick portals and other host-OS services into treating the Flatpak app as though it was an ordinary, non-sandboxed host-OS process. They can do this by manipulating the VFS using recent mount-related syscalls that are not blocked by Flatpak's denylist seccomp filter, in order to substitute a crafted `/.flatpak-info` or make that file disappear entirely. Flatpak apps that act as clients for AF_UNIX sockets such as those used by Wayland, Pipewire or pipewire-pulse can escalate the privileges that the corresponding services will believe the Flatpak app has. Note that protocols that operate entirely over the D-Bus session bus (user bus), system bus or accessibility bus are not affected by this. This is due to the use of a proxy process `xdg-dbus-proxy`, whose VFS cannot be manipulated by the Flatpak app, when interacting with these buses. Patches exist for versions 1.10.4 and 1.12.0, and as of time of publication, a patch for version 1.8.2 is being planned. There are no workarounds aside from upgrading to a patched version.

code_snippets:
if (r < 0 && r == -EFAULT /* unknown syscall */)
        return flatpak_fail_error (error, FLATPAK_ERROR_SETUP_FAILED, _("Failed to block syscall %d"), scall);
vulnerabilities.description: The error check after seccomp_rule_add is logically inverted. The comment says -EFAULT means 'unknown syscall' (which should be the non-fatal case to ignore), but the code only returns an error when r IS -EFAULT, silently ignoring all other failure codes (e.g., -EINVAL, -ENOMEM). This means if seccomp_rule_add fails for a real reason, the blocklist rule is silently not applied and execution continues, resulting in an incomplete seccomp filter that does not block dangerous syscalls.

code_snippets:
if (r < 0 && r == -EFAULT /* unknown syscall */)
        return flatpak_fail_error (error, FLATPAK_ERROR_SETUP_FAILED, _("Failed to block syscall %d"), scall);

step_by_step_execution.description: seccomp_rule_add is called to add a blocklist rule for a syscall (e.g., mount, chroot, clone with CLONE_NEWUSER).

state.value: return value of seccomp_rule_add, could be any negative error

step_by_step_execution.description: seccomp_rule_add returns a failure code other than -EFAULT, for example -EINVAL or -ENOMEM.
The error check 'r < 0 && r == -EFAULT' is evaluated. Since r is not -EFAULT, the condition is false, and the error is silently ignored.
Execution continues with the next iteration of the loop. The dangerous syscall (e.g., mount) was NOT added to the seccomp blocklist, but no error is raised.

state.value: missing the rule for the failed syscall

step_by_step_execution.description: The incomplete seccomp filter is exported and applied to the sandbox. The sandboxed process can now invoke the syscall that should have been blocked.

state.value: applied but incomplete — missing critical blocklist entries

commit_message:
Properly validate sparse tensor in `SparseTensorSliceDataset`

Existing validation was incomplete.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 415375048
Change-Id: I14cd18f29ede73286f3ffac35171bd15828997e9
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `SparseTensorSliceDataset` has an undefined behavior: under certain condition it can be made to dereference a `nullptr` value. The 3 input arguments to `SparseTensorSliceDataset` represent a sparse tensor. However, there are some preconditions that these arguments must satisfy but these are not validated in the implementation. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

commit_message: fixed #1902
cve_desc: A Segmentation fault caused by a null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via the gf_avc_parse_nalu function in av_parsers.c when using mp4box, which causes a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.description: After checking that `avc->s_info.sps` is not NULL, the code dereferences `n_state.sps->poc_type` without verifying that `n_state.sps` is non-NULL. The `n_state` struct is initially copied from `avc->s_info`, but `avc_parse_slice(bs, avc, idr_flag, &n_state)` is called before this check and may set `n_state.sps` to NULL (e.g., if the parsed slice references an SPS index that hasn't been loaded). This leads to a NULL pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function entry: `n_state` is copied from `avc->s_info` via memcpy.

state.value: same as avc->s_info.sps (could be any value)

step_by_step_execution.description: NAL unit type is parsed from the bitstream header. Suppose it is GF_AVC_NALU_NON_IDR_SLICE or GF_AVC_NALU_IDR_SLICE, so `slice` is set to 1.

`avc_parse_slice(bs, avc, idr_flag, &n_state)` is called. This function parses slice header data from the bitstream and may update `n_state.sps` based on the SPS ID referenced by the PPS. If the referenced SPS is not yet loaded, `n_state.sps` could be set to NULL.

state.value: potentially NULL (if referenced SPS not loaded)

step_by_step_execution.description: The code checks `if (!avc->s_info.sps) return -1;` — this validates only `avc->s_info.sps`, not `n_state.sps`. Suppose `avc->s_info.sps` is non-NULL (from a previous valid slice).
The code dereferences `n_state.sps->poc_type` in the comparison `avc->s_info.sps->poc_type == n_state.sps->poc_type`, causing a NULL pointer dereference.

commit_message:
[lite] Update TfLiteIntArrayCreate to return size_t

PiperOrigin-RevId: 416439896
Change-Id: I847f69b68d1ddaff4b1e925a09b8b69c1756653b
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. An attacker can craft a TFLite model that would cause an integer overflow in `TfLiteIntArrayCreate`. The `TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes` returns an `int` instead of a `size_t. An attacker can control model inputs such that `computed_size` overflows the size of `int` datatype. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: Integer overflow when computing the allocation size. If 'size' is a large positive value (or negative), the multiplication `sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * size` can overflow a 32-bit int, wrapping around to a small or negative value. The caller would then allocate a much smaller buffer than expected, leading to subsequent out-of-bounds writes when the buffer is used to store 'size' elements.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function receives 'size' as an int parameter. No validation is performed on 'size'.
The multiplication sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * size is computed. With sizeof(int)==4 and size==0x40000000, this yields 4 * 0x40000000 = 0x100000000, which overflows a 32-bit int to 0.
computed_size is assigned sizeof(dummy) + 0, resulting in a very small value (e.g. 8 bytes for struct overhead) instead of the ~4GB that would be needed.

state.value: sizeof(dummy) (e.g. 8), far smaller than needed for 1073741824 elements

step_by_step_execution.description: The small computed_size is returned to the caller, which will allocate a tiny buffer. When the caller then tries to populate it with 'size' (1073741824) integers, a heap buffer overflow occurs.

state.value: ~8, used by caller to allocate a buffer that is too small

commit_message: fixed #1902
cve_desc: A Segmentation fault caused by a null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in Gpac through 1.0.1 via the gf_avc_parse_nalu function in av_parsers.c when using mp4box, which causes a denial of service.

vulnerabilities.description: In release/production builds, `assert()` is compiled out (defined as a no-op). If `avc->s_info.sps` or `n_state.sps` is NULL when execution reaches the poc_type comparison, a NULL pointer dereference occurs. The code relies solely on an assert to guard against NULL, providing no real protection in non-debug builds.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function entry: `n_state` is copied from `avc->s_info`. If `avc->s_info.sps` is NULL at entry, `n_state.sps` will also be NULL.
NAL header is read and `n_state.nal_unit_type` is set. Suppose it is `GF_AVC_NALU_NON_IDR_SLICE`, entering the slice parsing case.
`avc_parse_slice` is called. Suppose it returns 0 (success). `ret` is set to 0.

The condition `(avc->s_info.nal_unit_type > GF_AVC_NALU_IDR_SLICE) || (avc->s_info.nal_unit_type < GF_AVC_NALU_NON_IDR_SLICE)` is checked. If `avc->s_info.nal_unit_type` was previously set to a valid slice type (e.g. GF_AVC_NALU_NON_IDR_SLICE), this condition is false, so execution continues.
The frame_num, field_pic_flag, and nal_ref_idc comparisons all evaluate to no difference (ret remains 0), so execution falls through to `assert(avc->s_info.sps)`.
In a release build, `assert(avc->s_info.sps)` is a no-op. Execution proceeds to dereference `avc->s_info.sps->poc_type` and `n_state.sps->poc_type`, causing a NULL pointer dereference.

commit_message:
Fix empty resource handle vulnerability.

Some ops that attempt to extract a resource handle from user input
can lead to nullptr dereferences.  This returns an error in such
a case.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 445571938
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, multiple TensorFlow operations misbehave in eager mode when the resource handle provided to them is invalid. In graph mode, it would have been impossible to perform these API calls, but migration to TF 2.x eager mode opened up this vulnerability. If the resource handle is empty, then a reference is bound to a null pointer inside TensorFlow codebase (various codepaths). This is undefined behavior. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

commit_message:
[lite] Update TfLiteIntArrayCreate to return size_t

PiperOrigin-RevId: 416439896
Change-Id: I847f69b68d1ddaff4b1e925a09b8b69c1756653b
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. An attacker can craft a TFLite model that would cause an integer overflow in `TfLiteIntArrayCreate`. The `TfLiteIntArrayGetSizeInBytes` returns an `int` instead of a `size_t. An attacker can control model inputs such that `computed_size` overflows the size of `int` datatype. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: If `size` is a large positive value or a negative value, the multiplication `sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * size` can overflow or wrap around. Since `size` is a signed `int`, a negative value will be implicitly converted to `size_t` (unsigned), resulting in a very large number, causing the computed size to wrap around. This can lead to an undersized allocation by callers, followed by out-of-bounds writes.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is called with a negative or very large `size` parameter, e.g. size = -1.

state.value: -1 (or any negative int / large positive int)

step_by_step_execution.description: `size` is implicitly converted to `size_t` (unsigned) for the multiplication. For size=-1, this becomes SIZE_MAX (e.g., 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF on 64-bit).
The multiplication `sizeof(dummy.data[0]) * (size_t)size` wraps around due to unsigned overflow, producing a smaller-than-expected value.
Adding `sizeof(dummy)` to the wrapped result causes further wraparound, producing a small final value.

state.value: a small value due to unsigned wraparound

step_by_step_execution.description: The caller uses the returned small size to allocate memory, then writes `size` elements into `dummy.data[]`, causing a heap buffer overflow.

state.value: small allocation size, but caller writes far beyond it

commit_message: fixed #1905
cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a null pointer dereference vulnerability in the mpgviddmx_process function in reframe_mpgvid.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service. This vulnerability is possibly due to an incomplete fix for CVE-2021-40566.

vulnerabilities.description: When `ctx->bytes_in_header` is small (e.g., 1), `8 - ctx->bytes_in_header` evaluates to 7, but `remain` could be as small as 5 (just enough to pass the `remain < 5` check). This causes an out-of-bounds read of up to 2 bytes past the valid `start` buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: In a previous invocation of the while loop, `remain` was less than 5 (e.g., 1), so the bytes are stored in `ctx->hdr_store` and `ctx->bytes_in_header` is set to `remain`.
On the next call to `mpgviddmx_process`, a new packet is obtained. The packet may be small. After setup, the while loop is entered.

state.value: points to buffer with exactly 5 valid bytes remaining

step_by_step_execution.description: The `remain < 5` check passes since remain equals 5, so execution continues.
The `ctx->bytes_in_header` block is entered because `ctx->bytes_in_header` is non-zero (1). The code copies `8 - ctx->bytes_in_header = 8 - 1 = 7` bytes from `start` into `ctx->hdr_store`.
The memcpy reads 7 bytes from `start`, but only 5 bytes are valid in the buffer pointed to by `start`, resulting in a 2-byte out-of-bounds read.

commit_message:
run: Don't allow chroot()

If we don't allow pivot_root() then there seems no reason why we should
allow chroot().

Partially fixes GHSA-67h7-w3jq-vh4q.

Flatpak apps that act as clients for AF_UNIX sockets such as those used by Wayland, Pipewire or pipewire-pulse can escalate the privileges that the corresponding services will believe the Flatpak app has. code_snippets:
if (r < 0 && r == -EFAULT /* unknown syscall */)
        return flatpak_fail_error (error, FLATPAK_ERROR_SETUP_FAILED, _("Failed to block syscall %d"), scall);
vulnerabilities.description: The error check after seccomp_rule_add uses `r == -EFAULT` which is almost certainly the wrong error code (unknown syscalls typically yield -EDOM in libseccomp). More critically, any failure return code other than -EFAULT is silently ignored, meaning if seccomp_rule_add fails (e.g., -ENOMEM, -EINVAL, -EOPNOTSUPP), the dangerous syscall will NOT be blocked in the filter, but execution continues as if it was. This weakens the sandbox by allowing blocklisted syscalls to pass through.

code_snippets:
if (r < 0 && r == -EFAULT /* unknown syscall */)
        return flatpak_fail_error (error, FLATPAK_ERROR_SETUP_FAILED, _("Failed to block syscall %d"), scall);

step_by_step_execution.description: seccomp_rule_add is called to add a blocklist rule for a syscall (e.g., clone with CLONE_NEWUSER argument filter, or ioctl with TIOCSTI).
seccomp_rule_add returns a negative error code that is not -EFAULT, such as -EINVAL or -ENOMEM, indicating the rule was NOT added to the filter.
The error check `r < 0 && r == -EFAULT` evaluates to false because r is not -EFAULT. The error is silently ignored and execution continues to the next iteration.
The seccomp filter is exported and applied without the failed rule. The dangerous syscall (e.g., clone with CLONE_NEWUSER, or ioctl with TIOCSTI for CVE-2017-5226) is not blocked in the sandbox.

state.value: missing blocklist entry for the failed syscall

commit_message:
Fix empty resource handle vulnerability.

Some ops that attempt to extract a resource handle from user input
can lead to nullptr dereferences.  This returns an error in such
a case.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 445571938
cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, multiple TensorFlow operations misbehave in eager mode when the resource handle provided to them is invalid. In graph mode, it would have been impossible to perform these API calls, but migration to TF 2.x eager mode opened up this vulnerability. If the resource handle is empty, then a reference is bound to a null pointer inside TensorFlow codebase (various codepaths). This is undefined behavior. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

commit_message: Fix overflow of triggered topics
cve_desc: uWebSockets 18.11.0 and 18.12.0 has a stack-based buffer overflow in uWS::TopicTree::trimTree (called from uWS::TopicTree::unsubscribeAll). NOTE: the vendor's position is that this is "a minor issue or not even an issue at all" because the developer of an application (that uses uWebSockets) should not be allowing the large number of triggered topics to accumulate

vulnerabilities.description: Out-of-bounds write on the `triggeredTopics` array (apparently sized 64). The overflow check `numTriggeredTopics == 64` only fires once at the top of the function. Within a single invocation, multiple loop iterations can each increment `numTriggeredTopics` (via `terminatingWildcardChild` entries), plus the final exact-match entry, without any bounds re-check. Additionally, the recursive call for `wildcardChild` can further increment the counter. If `numTriggeredTopics` enters the function at 63 (or even lower with enough topic segments), the repeated increments write past the end of the 64-element `triggeredTopics` buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is entered with numTriggeredTopics == 63 (less than 64, so the drain() guard is not triggered).
The loop begins iterating over topic segments. Suppose the topic is 'a/b/c' and each intermediate node has a non-null, not-yet-triggered terminatingWildcardChild.
First iteration: iterator->terminatingWildcardChild is non-null and not triggered, so triggeredTopics[63] is written and numTriggeredTopics becomes 64.
Second iteration (segment 'b'): iterator->terminatingWildcardChild is non-null and not triggered. triggeredTopics[64] is written — this is out-of-bounds. numTriggeredTopics becomes 65.
Third iteration (segment 'c'): another out-of-bounds write at triggeredTopics[65]. numTriggeredTopics becomes 66.
After the loop, the exact-match entry writes triggeredTopics[66], yet another out-of-bounds access.

commit_message:
Validate `proto.dtype()` before calling `set_dtype()`.

This prevents a `DCHECK`-fail when the proto contains an invalid dtype for a tensor shape with 0 elements or for an incomplete tensor shape.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 408369083
Change-Id: Ia21a3e3d62a90d642a4561f08f3b543e5ad00c46
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. When decoding a tensor from protobuf, a TensorFlow process can encounter cases where a `CHECK` assertion is invalidated based on user controlled arguments, if the tensors have an invalid `dtype` and 0 elements or an invalid shape. This allows attackers to cause denial of services in TensorFlow processes. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

code_snippets:
TensorShape shape(proto.tensor_shape());
  const int64_t N = shape.num_elements();
vulnerabilities.description: Integer overflow in shape.num_elements(). If the TensorShape dimensions are very large, their product can overflow int64_t, wrapping around to a small positive value. TensorShape::IsValid checks the shape proto structure but may not fully guard against overflow in the product of all dimensions. If N overflows to a small positive number, a small buffer is allocated via Helper<T>::Decode or FromProtoField, but shape_ is set to the original large shape. Subsequent code that accesses the tensor based on its shape will read/write out of bounds of the undersized buffer.

code_snippets:
CASES_WITH_DEFAULT(proto.dtype(), p = Helper<T>::Decode(a, content, N),
                         dtype_error = true, dtype_error = true);

step_by_step_execution.description: TensorShape::IsValid checks the proto shape, but does not fully prevent integer overflow in the product of dimensions.

step_by_step_execution.description: TensorShape is constructed from the proto and num_elements() computes the product of dimensions, which overflows int64_t.

state.value: Small positive value due to integer overflow (e.g. the wrapped-around product of 2^31 * 2^31 * 2)

step_by_step_execution.description: Since N > 0 and proto.dtype() is nonzero, the allocation branch is taken. Helper<T>::Decode allocates a buffer sized for the small overflowed N elements.

state.value: Pointer to a small buffer (sized for overflowed N elements)

step_by_step_execution.description: shape_ is set to the original (large) shape, and buf_ is set to the undersized buffer p. The tensor now has a large reported shape but a small backing buffer.

state.value: Shape with very large number of elements (e.g. 2^63)
Pointer to small buffer allocated for overflowed N

step_by_step_execution.description: Any subsequent access to the tensor using its shape for indexing will read or write beyond the allocated buffer, causing a heap-based buffer overflow.

commit_message:
Prevent use after free in `DecodePng` kernel.

We are cleaning up the memory in `decode` and then we are using an `OP_REQUIRES` to check an invariant on the `decode` data.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 409299145
Change-Id: I4eb93aaca52483eb202e89b78df07fbb2f6cb254
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. A malicious user can cause a use after free behavior when decoding PNG images. After `png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode)` gets called, the values of `decode.width` and `decode.height` are in an unspecified state. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

code_snippets:
std::unique_ptr<uint16[]> buffer(
          new uint16[height * width * decode.channels]);
vulnerabilities.description: Integer overflow in buffer size calculation. `height`, `width`, and `decode.channels` are all `int` types. The check ensures `total_size = width * height < 2^29`, but the allocation computes `height * width * decode.channels` as a 32-bit int multiplication. With `decode.channels` up to 4, the product can reach 2^31, overflowing a signed 32-bit int. This undefined behavior can result in a smaller-than-expected allocation, leading to a heap buffer overflow when `CommonFinishDecode` writes decoded image data into the undersized buffer.

code_snippets:
std::unique_ptr<uint16[]> buffer(
          new uint16[height * width * decode.channels]);

step_by_step_execution.description: A crafted PNG image is provided with dimensions and channel count that pass the size checks but cause integer overflow during buffer allocation. For example, width=16384 (2^14), height=32768 (2^15), channels=4.

Width and height are cast to int and validated. width=16384, height=32768. total_size = 16384 * 32768 = 536870912 (2^29). The check `total_size >= (1LL << 29)` uses `>=`, so total_size = 536870912 = 2^29 would fail. Use height=32767 instead: total_size = 16384*32767 = 536854528 < 2^29. All dimension checks pass.

data_type_ is DT_FLOAT, so the code enters the float branch and computes `height * width * decode.channels` as int arithmetic: 32767 * 16384 * 4 = 2147418112. This is close to INT_MAX. With slightly adjusted values (e.g. width=16384, height=32768 wouldn't pass, but other combinations near the boundary can overflow). For a cleaner example: width=23170, height=23170 gives total_size=536848900 < 2^29, and 23170*23170*4 = 2147395600*... actually let me use width=32768, height=16384-1=16383, channels=4: 32768*16383=536838144 < 2^29. 32768*16383*4 = 2147352576, still fits. The exact overflow occurs when total_size is just under 2^29 and channels=4, pushing the product to ~2^31.

state.value: Close to or exceeding INT_MAX (2^31-1), causing signed integer overflow (undefined behavior)

step_by_step_execution.description: Due to signed integer overflow (undefined behavior), the size passed to `new uint16[...]` may be a small or negative value interpreted as a huge size_t, or a wrapped-around small positive value, resulting in an undersized heap buffer allocation.

state.value: Incorrect due to integer overflow — potentially much smaller than needed

step_by_step_execution.description: CommonFinishDecode writes the full decoded image data (height * width * channels * sizeof(uint16) bytes) into the undersized buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow.

state.value: ~2 GB of decoded pixel data written to undersized buffer

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `SparseCountSparseOutput` is vulnerable to a heap overflow. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: The int64_t value from indices_values(idx, 0) is truncated to an int. A large positive int64_t value (e.g. 2147483648) becomes a negative int (e.g. INT_MIN). The subsequent check `batch >= num_batches` does NOT catch negative batch values (e.g. -2147483648 >= 2 is false), allowing a negative index to be used in per_batch_counts[batch], which is an out-of-bounds access on the underlying vector.

step_by_step_execution.description: The shape tensor has 2 elements, so is_1d is false. shape_vector(0) is a small value like 2, so num_batches = 2.
per_batch_counts is created as a BatchedMap<W> with num_batches (2) entries.
In the loop, indices_values(idx, 0) returns an int64_t value of e.g. 2147483648 (0x80000000). This is truncated to int, producing -2147483648 (INT_MIN).
The guard check evaluates: batch >= num_batches → -2147483648 >= 2 → false. The check does NOT trigger, so execution continues past the OP_REQUIRES.

commit_message:
Replace faulty overflow check with a builder for `TensorShape`.

Prevents an integer overflow that was not caught before.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 415381595
Change-Id: I76585ddedc912bd9f4a390aeafa8e2ced1a28863
cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `AddManySparseToTensorsMap` is vulnerable to an integer overflow which results in a `CHECK`-fail when building new `TensorShape` objects (so, an assert failure based denial of service). We are missing some validation on the shapes of the input tensors as well as directly constructing a large `TensorShape` with user-provided dimensions. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

vulnerabilities.description: The overflow check uses an `int` (32-bit) variable `new_num_elements` to store the result of `MultiplyWithoutOverflow`, which operates on and returns `int64_t` values. When the 64-bit result is implicitly truncated to 32-bit `int`, a large positive 64-bit value can become a small positive 32-bit value, causing the `< 0` overflow check to be bypassed. This allows a SparseTensor with an extremely large shape (whose element count overflows 32 bits but not 64 bits, or overflows 64 bits but truncates to a positive 32-bit value) to pass validation, potentially leading to downstream memory issues when `TensorShape` is constructed from the unchecked shape.

step_by_step_execution.description: User provides `input_shape` as a vector of int64 values, e.g., [2, 2147483648, 2] (shape dimensions).
The overflow check loop begins with `new_num_elements` as a 32-bit `int` initialized to 1.
First iteration (i=0): `MultiplyWithoutOverflow(1, 2)` returns `int64_t(2)`. This is truncated to `int(2)`. Check `2 < 0` is false.

Second iteration (i=1): `MultiplyWithoutOverflow(2, 2147483648)` — but the first argument is implicitly widened from `int(2)` to `int64_t(2)`. The result is `int64_t(4294967296)` (2^32). This is truncated to `int`: `4294967296 % 2^32 = 0`. Check `0 < 0` is false.
Third iteration (i=2): `MultiplyWithoutOverflow(0, 2)` returns `int64_t(0)`, truncated to `int(0)`. Check `0 < 0` is false.

The overflow check passes (overflow_ocurred is false), even though the true product of shape dimensions is 2*2147483648*2 = 8589934592, which overflows a 32-bit integer. Execution continues to create a TensorShape from the unvalidated large shape.

state.value: TensorShape([2, 2147483648, 2]) — constructed without proper overflow validation

cve_desc: Buffer overflow in the send_control_msg function in hw/char/virtio-serial-bus.c in QEMU before 2.4.0 allows guest users to cause a denial of service (QEMU process crash) via a crafted virtio control message.

vulnerabilities.description: The function copies 'len' bytes into the guest-provided scatter-gather buffers without checking that the total size of elem.in_sg is sufficient to hold 'len' bytes. If the guest provides a buffer smaller than 'len', this results in writing beyond the guest's buffer boundaries, potentially corrupting host or guest memory. The TODO comment in the code acknowledges this missing check.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function is called with a buf and len representing a control message to send to the guest.

state.value: size of the control message, e.g. sizeof(struct virtio_console_control)

step_by_step_execution.description: The virtqueue is checked for readiness and an element is popped from the queue. The guest controls the size of elem.in_sg buffers.

state.value: guest-provided scatter-gather list with potentially small total buffer size

step_by_step_execution.description: iov_from_buf copies 'len' bytes into elem.in_sg without verifying that the total iov size >= len. If the guest provided a buffer smaller than len, iov_from_buf may write past the allocated iov buffer boundaries or the reported length pushed back via virtqueue_push is incorrect, leading to guest memory corruption or information leakage.

not checked, could be less than len

vulnerabilities.description: When argc == 9 and argv[8] equals "*", arg_start is set to 9. The code then dereferences argv[arg_start][0] (i.e., argv[9][0]) without checking that arg_start < argc, causing an out-of-bounds read. Valid argv indices are 0 through argc-1 (i.e., 0 through 8).

The check `if (argc < 8)` passes (argc is not less than 8), so execution continues.
